case_id: Dynamic Metal Stamping process with parametric scrap_rate

pi_items.source: Task description (task.json lines 17 and description line 2) specifies creation of a process with this exact name. Verified in export_result.sh line 76 which queries: WHERE LOWER(NAME) LIKE '%dynamic metal%'
Task description specifies 'Define a local Input Parameter named scrap_rate with a default value of 0.2 (20%)'. Verified in export_result.sh line 85 which queries: WHERE NAME = 'scrap_rate'
Verified in setup_task.sh lines 18-19 and export_result.sh lines 24-26 which reference this exact path. The /home/ga/LCA_Results directory is created in setup_task.sh line 22
Verified in setup_task.sh lines 18-19 and export_result.sh lines 24-26 which reference this exact path
Task description specifies formulas: '1 / (1 - scrap_rate)' and 'scrap_rate / (1 - scrap_rate)'. Verified in export_result.sh line 95 which queries: WHERE FORMULA LIKE '%scrap_rate%'

USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) Database is a real, publicly available database maintained by NREL at lcacommons.gov. Contains 550-660+ processes covering fuels, transport, metals, chemicals, resins, paper, and building materials. See nlr.gov/analysis/lci and lcacommons.gov

OpenLCA documentation (greendelta.github.io/openLCA2-manual/parameters/parameters.html) confirms local input parameters are supported within processes. Exchange schema supports both 'amount' and 'amount_formula' properties allowing formulas like '1 / (1 - scrap_rate)'

Task description specifies 'default value of 0.2 (20%)'. Mathematical verification: With 20% scrap rate, steel input = 1/(1-0.2) = 1.25 kg. This formula is correct for material balance where 1 kg finished part + scrap = input

pi_items.metadata_value: Higher scrap rate (20%) requires more input, should have higher impact
pi_items.verified_value: Higher scrap rate (20%) requires more input (1.25 kg) vs 10% requires (1.11 kg), producing higher material impact
pi_items.source: Mathematical verification: 1/(1-0.2)=1.25, 1/(1-0.1)=1.11. Verifier logic (verifier.py line 120) expects val_20 > val_10 * 1.05, confirming this relationship

privileged_info_summary: The parametric_yield_modeling task uses the real, publicly available USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) Database maintained by NREL, containing 550-660+ processes covering metals and other materials (lcacommons.gov). The task requires creating a new 'Dynamic Metal Stamping' process with a parametric 'scrap_rate' input parameter (default 0.2 for 20%) and flow formulas: steel_input = 1/(1-scrap_rate) and scrap_output = scrap_rate/(1-scrap_rate). OpenLCA documentation confirms support for local parameters and formula amounts in exchanges (greendelta.github.io). The formulas correctly implement material balance for metal stamping with parameterized scrap losses. Results are exported to /home/ga/LCA_Results/ as CSV files for two parameter values (20% and 10% scrap rates).
